title: feat: private/public visibility for community models (standalone)
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
Extracts the visibility slice onto current main, independent of the agents work.

- Adds `visibility` (`private | public`) to community endpoints; new models default private and existing models are backfilled public without changing their prices
- Any authenticated owner can register and use private models
- Private models are owner-only and appear in model catalogs only when authenticated with the owner API key
- Allowlisted owners can select Public during creation or later editing; public models may be free (`0`) or owner-priced
- Public models are globally callable/listed; priced usage retains the existing community-owner reward
- Dashboard shows the same Private/Public control on create and edit; Public is disabled with an approval explanation for non-allowlisted owners
- Polli CLI supports visibility and pricing on both create and update
- Excludes agent kinds, capability flags, tool pricing, uploaded source, prompt agents, worker deployment, and minimum-price policy

Validation: community endpoint + tracking integration tests 33/33; Gen and Enter typechecks; Drizzle migration check; Biome clean; migration dry-run preserves zero and positive legacy prices.

## Review hardening

- Endpoint probes are allowlist-gated and refuse redirects, preventing a validated base URL from redirecting to an unvalidated destination
- Updates persist visibility + the complete effective price set atomically under concurrent updates
- Community price definitions keep explicit zero rates; free private/public calls bill and reward 0 without missing-conversion-rate warnings
- Zero-priced community models bypass the balance preflight; paid public models retain normal budget and balance checks

## Known behavior

- Visibility changes propagate to gen within ≤60s (per-isolate registry cache TTL, pre-existing design)

## Rollout

1. `npm run migrate:production` (adds `visibility`, backfills existing rows public without changing prices) — staging already migrated
2. Deploy enter + gen
3. C

gist.headline: A little privacy hedge
gist.blurb: Community models can now grow behind a private garden wall or bloom in the public catalog. Owners get a proper workshop before opening the gate.
gist.summary: Community endpoints now support private and public visibility: new models default to owner-only private access, while allowlisted owners can publish globally callable models with either free (0) or owner-set pricing through the dashboard or Polli CLI.
gist.impact: Model builders can register and test private models with their own API key before publishing, while public models remain discoverable to everyone and retain the existing community-owner reward flow for priced usage.
gist.visual_concept: A cozy community-model garden with two neighboring plots: a private plot behind a small hedge and owner-key gate, and a public plot opening onto a shared path. Polly acts as the observant gardener, using a tiny tool belt to tend code-plants while a CRT monitor robot helps display Private/Public controls; flowering model capsules and small price tags represent free and owner-priced public models.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of a community model garden with one code-plant safely behind a small hedge and key-marked gate labeled "PRIVATE," beside a blooming shared plot labeled "PUBLIC" with tiny 0 and pollen price tags; Polly, the cute pixel art bee mascot with a yellow body, dark brown horizontal stripes, black oval eyes, bright pink rectangular cheek blushes, small happy smile, light blue translucent wings, and a tiny gardener tool belt, carefully opens the public garden gate while a friendly CRT monitor robot holds a settings panel. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background, chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike, with flowers and vines around the code-plants, nostalgic but beautiful.
